title: Anna Howard Shaw Day

jokes.text: Valentine's Day is a sham created by card companies to reinforce and exploit gender stereotypes.
jokes.setup: Normal Valentine's cookie sales pitch from coworker
jokes.punchline: Liz's overly academic feminist takedown of Valentine's Day
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon character moment - turning simple holiday into academic critique. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: Learn from my sexual misadventures, Evelyn.
jokes.setup: Talking to innocent young girl selling cookies
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate adult advice to child
jokes.explanation: Good economy and character work. Liz's tendency to overshare with inappropriate audiences.

jokes.text: Last Valentine's Day, I watched my boyfriend, Drew's, mother/grandmother die.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions watching someone die, plus the mother/grandmother confusion
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered casually. The mother/grandmother detail adds absurdist layer.

jokes.text: I met Floyd on Valentine's Day, but he left me for the city of Cleveland.
jokes.punchline: Personifying Cleveland as romantic rival
jokes.explanation: Clever framing of geographic relocation as romantic betrayal. Good economy.

jokes.text: Instead, these cookies celebrate the February 14th birthday of Anna Howard Shaw, famed American suffragette.
jokes.punchline: Creating elaborate alternate holiday for obscure historical figure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment. Peak Liz - overly intellectual, feminist alternative. Episode title reference.

jokes.text: No, C.N.B.C. gives me a headache. I get all my money advice from P.B.S.
jokes.setup: Jack asking about CNBC financial show
jokes.punchline: Liz gets investment advice from educational TV
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Liz's anti-corporate, PBS-loving personality.

jokes.text: Where should I put my money? In tech stocks? Or the housing market? Tech stocks, Foxy Moneybags! Tech stocks!
jokes.setup: Claims to get advice from PBS
jokes.punchline: Imaginary PBS host giving stock tips with ridiculous name
jokes.explanation: Absurd PBS financial advisor name 'Foxy Moneybags' is perfect 30 Rock wordplay.

jokes.text: Like when you think there's one more stair, but there isn't. And all of a sudden, you're like 'Whoa!'
jokes.punchline: Comparing emotional trauma to minor physical mishap
jokes.explanation: Perfect analogy - relatable physical experience for complex emotion. Great structure and economy.

jokes.text: Is that sex, Lemon? It's the way I do it.
jokes.punchline: Liz's deadpan clarification about her sexual style
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two jokes in few words. Classic Liz oversharing disguised as clarification.

jokes.text: Perhaps a, uh, bra with a front clasp.
jokes.punchline: Overly specific underwear detail shared with employee
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Jack's inappropriate oversharing. Specific detail makes it memorable.

jokes.text: I scheduled a root canal for February 14th, Jack. I will spend half the day in twilight sleep.
jokes.punchline: Liz's deliberately miserable alternative
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz - choosing medical procedure over romance. Self-destructive but logical to her.

jokes.text: My Stepson is My Cyber-Husband
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake Lifetime movie title
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of Lifetime movie sensationalism. Great wordplay combining modern and taboo elements.

jokes.text: You are truly the Picasso of loneliness.
jokes.punchline: Jack's artistic comparison for Liz's romantic failures
jokes.explanation: Elegant insult disguised as compliment. Perfect Jack-style backhanded praise.

jokes.text: Or I am that painting elephant of being awesome.
jokes.punchline: Liz's confused but confident comeback
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Liz missing the insult and creating nonsensical comeback.

jokes.text: But if Beyonce simply answered one of my letters, I'd stop trying to break into her house.
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to stalking Beyonce herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism - complains about stalker while being one herself.

jokes.text: Maynard's debilitating devotion validates how wonderful I am.
jokes.punchline: Twisted logic making harassment about self-validation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - Jenna's narcissism turning harassment into ego boost.

jokes.text: Maybe to one of my peers, like Julia Roberts.
jokes.punchline: Considering Julia Roberts a peer
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna delusion about her status. Good economy in revealing character.

jokes.text: Would a fake woman have a personal website at JDLutz.com/karen/proof?
jokes.punchline: Website URL suggesting elaborate fake girlfriend construction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lutz pathetic-ness. The specific URL structure makes it funnier.

jokes.text: My cousin set me up on a blind date for Valentine's, and I just found out the girl is... well, urban.
jokes.punchline: Awkward euphemism for race
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate casual racism. The pause before 'urban' sells the discomfort.

jokes.text: You know that load with the messed-up teeth who cleans the urinals? She'll only go out with me if she can bring her ugly sister.
jokes.punchline: Incredibly ungallant description of potential dates
jokes.explanation: Good character work for Frank's crudeness but fairly predictable writing.

jokes.text: One word. Oral. Two words. Oral surgery.
jokes.punchline: Subverting sexual expectation with medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and structure. Perfect Liz anti-romantic sentiment with good setup/payoff.

jokes.text: When I had my wisdom teeth out, I tried to get in the bath with my mon.
jokes.punchline: Disturbing overshare about inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable overshare that fits character type but relies on shock value.

jokes.text: Then she takes the kids there and I stay home and get wasted in my garage. It's what keeps the magic alive.
jokes.punchline: Pathetic reality disguised as romantic advice
jokes.explanation: Good ironic structure - presents dysfunction as relationship advice.

jokes.text: And the food in his beard tells us that he purchased a snack pack on the train from New Haven.
jokes.punchline: Brutal specific observation about disheveled appearance
jokes.explanation: Excellent TV host meanness. Specific details make it more cutting and funny.

jokes.text: The Baltics or women's tennis.
jokes.punchline: Lumping women's tennis with geopolitical issue
jokes.explanation: Absurd juxtaposition shows Jack's casual dismissiveness. Good economy.

jokes.text: Mitt Romney's oldest son, Jezba.
jokes.punchline: Made-up name for Romney's son
jokes.explanation: Absurd fake name fits Jack's political knowledge mixed with nonsense.

jokes.text: Just sit a couple out, Stein, god.
jokes.setup: Walter trying to participate in rapid-fire segment
jokes.punchline: Brutal dismissal on live TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect TV host cruelty. Shows Avery's character efficiently.

jokes.text: Blackberry, Warren Buffett. iPhone, Jimmy Buffett.
jokes.punchline: Buffett name confusion based on device
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay and economy. Shows Jack's business connections with clever phone/name connection.

jokes.text: Tie, The Fountainhead or Uncle Buck.
jokes.punchline: Pairing Ayn Rand philosophy with John Candy comedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack contradiction - highbrow and lowbrow tastes. Great character insight.

jokes.text: On Valentine's Day, Angie and I rent a room with a heart-shaped hot tub and cook chili in it. Then we take it to a soup kitchen. And that's where it starts to get sexy.
jokes.punchline: Escalating weirdness ending with inappropriate location for romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy absurdism. Escalating bizarre behavior with inappropriate climax.

jokes.text: I will be attending an all-day abstinence rally. You're welcome to come. I think I've got an extra gender-neutralizing hood.
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's bizarre anti-sexual event with costume element
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth weirdness. 'Gender-neutralizing hood' is brilliant absurd detail.

jokes.text: Has the dog who gives you your orders died?
jokes.punchline: Casually referencing stalker's mental illness
jokes.explanation: Dark but character-appropriate. Jenna's casual cruelty about mental illness.

jokes.text: I just thought it would be with me in the trunk of a rental car.
jokes.punchline: Expected it to end in her murder
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor. Jenna's twisted romantic expectations perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: Oh, no, The Peace Corp. Lawrence Peace's corporation. We drilled for oil in gorilla habitats.
jokes.punchline: Corporate evil disguised as charity work
jokes.explanation: Perfect 30 Rock corporate satire. Great misdirection and escalation.

jokes.text: We are going to test poisons on you.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of animal cruelty
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about corporate evil. Fits Jack's casual amorality.

jokes.text: I think it started when I walked in on my parents doing it the day Reagan was shot.
jokes.punchline: Traumatic childhood memory tied to historical event
jokes.explanation: Good inappropriate overshare. Historical reference adds specificity.

jokes.text: One time, I ran over an old lady in Arizona and just kept driving.
jokes.setup: Jack saying he loves having secrets
jokes.punchline: Jonathan's casual confession to hit-and-run
jokes.explanation: Dark humor but relies heavily on shock value. Fits Jonathan's weird oversharing.

jokes.text: It's 3:30 in the morning in Geneva, Jack. Who's your call with? The hooker working the corner outside Raiffeisenbank?
jokes.punchline: Specific knowledge proving the lie with prostitute detail
jokes.explanation: Excellent call-out with specific financial knowledge. Shows Avery's intelligence.

jokes.text: And in Switzerland, that is, uh, 'Night Business Month'.
jokes.punchline: Jack's pathetic attempt to save face with made-up Swiss tradition
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's inability to admit defeat gracefully.

jokes.text: If you don't get that tooth fixed, the infection will probably move to your brain and kill you.
jokes.punchline: Casual prediction of Liz's death
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation fits character but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Then your ghost is going to see some disgusting stuff.
jokes.punchline: Frank's confidence in his grossness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment about Frank's self-awareness of his grossness.

jokes.text: 'mon se poivre' That means 'my salt and pepper' in Swiss French.
jokes.punchline: Jack's interpretation of flirtatious Swiss French
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier description. Shows developing relationship dynamic.

jokes.text: She was on Maxim's 'I'd Rape That 100.'
jokes.punchline: Liz's inappropriate magazine reference
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that relies on shock value. Fits Liz's inappropriate comments but edgy.

jokes.text: Meanwhile, the male escort that I hired to take me home from surgery has had a chlamydia flare-up, so...
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of hiring prostitute who has STD
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Liz's desperation. Shows how far she's fallen.

jokes.text: A not-stupid program that I came up with.
jokes.punchline: Jack's defensive pride in corporate program
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Jack's corporate pride and defensiveness.

jokes.text: Lemon, your hair looks very nice today. You should wear it like that more often.
jokes.setup: Jack in good mood from Avery interaction
jokes.punchline: Uncharacteristically nice comment to Liz
jokes.explanation: Subtle character moment showing Jack's good mood affects his treatment of others.

jokes.text: 'Jenna, I was in your bedroom last night. I left a gift in your toilet. You will be my bride someday.'
jokes.punchline: Escalating creepy behavior in romantic language
jokes.explanation: Good escalating creepiness. Shows the disturbing nature of stalking in dark comedy way.

jokes.text: Doug is my vibrator.
jokes.setup: Jenna mentioning longest relationship besides Doug
jokes.punchline: Revealing Doug is a sex toy
jokes.explanation: Simple shock humor but fits Jenna's oversharing. Kenneth's reaction makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Everything. Even zip up my own dress.
jokes.punchline: Example reveals how basic her accomplishments are
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's low standards for independence.

jokes.text: Not because you're black. Although it doesn't matter, because I'm black too. Nope, you're going to meet me. No, I'm not black.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to be supportive to doctor's office
jokes.punchline: Escalating racial awkwardness and confusion
jokes.explanation: Good cringe escalation showing Liz's tendency to make things worse when trying to be progressive.

jokes.text: Maybe someday, we'll live in a world where you ask us to pretend to be scientists.
jokes.setup: Asked to pretend to be bouncers
jokes.punchline: Calling out racial stereotyping in role assignments
jokes.explanation: Smart social commentary delivered with perfect deadpan timing. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Oh, sure. That sounds like an appropriate use of my time and talent.
jokes.setup: Jack asking Bon Jovi to impress date
jokes.punchline: Rock star's sarcastic response to being used as prop
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity frustration with corporate demands. Realistic character moment.

jokes.text: 'I, the undersigned, henceforth known as The Alone' hereby state that on February 14th, I have absolutely no one in my life.'
jokes.punchline: Brutal legal language about loneliness
jokes.explanation: Good legal document parody that makes isolation sound official and pathetic.

jokes.text: 'I, The Alone, otherwise known as Jane Sadwoman' release Dr. Kaplan's office of all legal responsibilities.
jokes.punchline: Additional humiliating alias
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and wordplay. 'Jane Sadwoman' is effective name pun.

jokes.text: 'Will likely die alone, eaten by house cats.'
jokes.punchline: Darkly specific prediction of lonely death
jokes.explanation: Dark but specific detail. Classic single woman stereotype taken to extreme.

jokes.text: That is the lamest thing I've heard since this. I'm Tyke Myson, baby boxer. I wrote that.
jokes.punchline: Reference to NBC's bad programming decisions
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about NBC programming. Bon Jovi's forced participation in bad content.

jokes.text: I'm Tyke Myson, baby boxer. I wrote that.
jokes.setup: Bon Jovi claims something is lamer than Liz's plan
jokes.punchline: He admits to writing something with terrible wordplay
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor with a terrible pun that's funny because it's so bad.

jokes.text: Because my boyfriend, Astronaut Mike Dexter, will be picking me up. On his motorcycle.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate and fake-sounding boyfriend details
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of fake boyfriend. 'Astronaut Mike Dexter' is perfect ridiculous name.

jokes.text: You know, I don't think the 'anastacia' actually 'affectored' me.
jokes.punchline: Made-up words showing drug effects while claiming no effect
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction - claiming no drug effects while clearly affected. Good made-up words.

jokes.text: You're all here because you didn't want me to be alone today.
jokes.punchline: Touching realization in altered state
jokes.explanation: Emotional beat that works because of drug context. Shows Liz's underlying desires.

jokes.text: Drew. So handsome. So, so stupid.
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of ex's attractiveness vs intelligence
jokes.explanation: Good economy and honesty. Repetition of 'so' emphasizes the stupidity.

jokes.text: Because I am a sailor on the sea of the human heart.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously overwrought metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect drug-induced overwrought poetry. Sounds profound but is ridiculous.

jokes.text: Okay, this bitch is tripping her ass off.
jokes.punchline: Frank's blunt assessment breaking romantic mood
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of romantic moment. Frank's crude honesty cuts through Liz's poetry.

jokes.text: Look, despite what happened in the shower last night, I'm a pretty traditional girl.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of shower sex while claiming traditionalism
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction between conservative values and wild behavior. Well-structured reveal.

jokes.text: A son I can throw a ball to and, when he's older, have power struggles with.
jokes.setup: Jack insisting he wants a family
jokes.punchline: His corporate view of father-son bonding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack - he can't even imagine parenthood without corporate power dynamics. The escalation to 'power struggles' is brilliant.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Who did this? I don't know. I guess some weirdo out there loves you.
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's innocent delivery of obviously his own work
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment. Kenneth's selfless act disguised as mystery. Good payoff.

jokes.text: Yes, you're a very pretty doggie.
jokes.punchline: Talking to Avery like she's a dog
jokes.explanation: Good drug effect humor. Shows Liz's altered perception in embarrassing way.

jokes.text: Bon Jovi.
jokes.setup: Avery asking what she thinks about Jack
jokes.punchline: Liz's random, drugged response
jokes.explanation: Perfect non-sequitur. The single word response that has nothing to do with the question shows her drug state effectively.

jokes.text: Shanice? What you going to do this weekend? Me going to re-wallpaper me powder room, man.
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical dialogue about home improvement
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of stereotypical TV representation. Not particularly sharp.

jokes.text: Don Cheadle on a bed of rice.
jokes.punchline: Treating celebrity name like menu item
jokes.explanation: Good absurd wordplay. Unexpected celebrity reference as food order.

jokes.text: Don Cheadle on a bed of rice.
jokes.setup: Being asked what to order for lunch
jokes.punchline: Treating a celebrity like a menu item
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - the specificity of Don Cheadle and 'bed of rice' like he's a fish dish. Random but perfectly structured.

jokes.text: Happy Valentine's Day, no one.
jokes.punchline: Liz's resigned acknowledgment of her situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending line. Encapsulates Liz's resigned acceptance with economy.

tmdb_overview: Liz schedules a root canal to avoid feeling lonely on Valentine's Day; Jack woos an attractive and successful TV host; Jenna's stalker loses interest.
insight: 72 jokes in 24 minutes hits peak 30 Rock density with character-driven cringe.
